development_velocity.github_activity: No public GitHub repositories found for getlinked.io

Absence of public reviews is notable and may indicate: (a) small customer base, (b) active suppression of review solicitation, (c) NDA-style relationships, or (d) clients in sensitive verticals (Crypto/Forex, gambling) preferring to avoid public visibility

Offices in Israel and Bulgaria. EU adequacy decisions apply to both countries per Privacy Policy. GDPR compliance implied by the adequacy framework reference.

SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, PCI DSS: not mentioned anywhere on website or in public records

growth_signals.evidence: AI-based lead scoring described in demo as 'a very, very nice feature' that is new and not yet populated with data — clearly in early rollout as of the demo date (2026-03-13 per demo data).
growth_signals.implication: Product team is actively investing in AI-layer features. This is a forward-looking capability that could differentiate them in a market where competitors (Scaleo, Everflow) already have ML-based traffic optimization.

growth_signals.signal: CRG (Conversion Rate Guarantee) automation is a differentiator
growth_signals.evidence: The 'Special Deals' module for CRG automation was highlighted as the first of several planned deal types, with the presenter noting more types will follow. This is a complex, high-value feature for Crypto/Forex networks.
growth_signals.implication: The company is building domain-specific financial automation for performance marketing — a defensible moat that generic affiliate platforms lack.

growth_signals.signal: Expanding beyond performance marketing into e-commerce
growth_signals.evidence: Dedicated landing page getlinked.io/getlinked-for-ecomm.php targets e-commerce operators, expanding the traditional Crypto/Forex affiliate network ICP.
growth_signals.implication: Market expansion into adjacent verticals (e-commerce affiliate programs) suggests the company is actively seeking to grow beyond its core niche.

stress_signals.evidence: No G2, Capterra, Trustpilot, or ProductHunt reviews found for getlinked.io. SoftwareWorld lists them as a product with alternatives but shows no review scores.
stress_signals.implication: Either the customer base is very small, customers are under NDA, or the company has not invested in social proof. This creates sales friction when prospects research the vendor independently — which this very demo appears to address by doing a live walkthrough instead of relying on external credibility.

stress_signals.evidence: The CRM dashboard was stuck on a loading spinner for approximately 13 minutes (frames 215s–1005s out of a 22-minute demo). A Cloudflare bot-protection challenge also interrupted the demo.
stress_signals.implication: Performance/reliability issue during a sales demo is a serious risk signal. Either the demo environment is poorly optimized, the platform has scalability issues, or the Cloudflare protection is misconfigured for demo use. This damages buyer confidence.

stress_signals.signal: No public changelog, roadmap, or release cadence
stress_signals.evidence: No public-facing release notes, changelog, or product roadmap found. Blog content is undated marketing copy rather than engineering updates.
stress_signals.implication: Opaque development process makes it hard for prospects to evaluate product velocity and trust that the platform will keep pace with their needs.

stress_signals.signal: Pricing is publicly available but was withheld during demo
stress_signals.evidence: Presenter told prospect 'I already sent you the pricing' and did not show pricing during demo. However, a public pricing page exists at getlinked.io/getlinked-pricing.php with specific plan tiers.
stress_signals.implication: The company appears to use a consultative/custom pricing approach in sales conversations even though standard pricing is publicly listed. This may indicate they negotiate pricing with prospects, or that the presenter was unaware the prospect could find pricing online independently.

strategic_intent.hypothesis: Getlinked is pursuing a high-touch, managed-service SaaS model targeting boutique and mid-market performance marketing operators who cannot afford a dedicated tech team
supporting_evidence: Every page of the website emphasizes the dedicated support team as a co-equal value driver alongside the software itself
Pricing starts at $250/m — accessible for small operators — with a dedicated team included rather than premium support tiers
'You can manage your network as a one-man show' is a key marketing message
The demo presenter handled the technical integrations pitch personally, reinforcing the service model

strategic_intent.hypothesis: The company is deliberately operating in verticals (Crypto/Forex, gambling) that mainstream SaaS platforms avoid, creating a defensible niche with less competition
supporting_evidence: CRG (Conversion Rate Guarantee) automation is specific to Forex/Crypto broker deal structures
FTD (First Time Deposit) tracking is a Forex/Crypto-native metric, not standard in e-commerce affiliate software
Demo prospect is explicitly in Crypto/Forex and testing gambling — exactly the vertical Getlinked is built for
No major SaaS review site (G2, Capterra) lists Getlinked — suggesting they grow via referral within regulated/grey verticals rather than inbound SEO

contradictions_with_recording.claimed_in_recording: Presenter said 'I already sent you the pricing' and declined to show pricing during the demo, implying pricing is custom/confidential
contradictions_with_recording.public_data_shows: A fully public pricing page exists at getlinked.io/getlinked-pricing.php with specific plan names and prices: Starter $250/m, Professional $750/m–$1,000/m, Enterprise $1,250/m–$2,000/m, all with a $500 one-time setup fee
contradictions_with_recording.implication: The presenter's behavior of withholding pricing during demo may be a sales tactic (anchoring, urgency creation) rather than actual confidentiality. The prospect could have found the public pricing page independently at any point. This also means the prospect had access to competitor comparison pricing before and after the demo.

contradictions_with_recording.claimed_in_recording: 'Setup fee paid upfront before onboarding. Recurring subscription (terms not disclosed in recording).'
contradictions_with_recording.public_data_shows: The setup fee is $500 (one-time, applied to all plans) — a very specific, publicly stated number
contradictions_with_recording.implication: The $500 setup fee is very low for an enterprise-grade white-label platform. It may function more as a commitment filter than a true cost recovery mechanism. This is important context for evaluating the total cost of ownership.

contradictions_with_recording.claimed_in_recording: Presenter implies a well-resourced company with full-time dedicated teams per client, 24/7 monitoring, and a capable support organization
contradictions_with_recording.public_data_shows: No LinkedIn company page found, no employee count data, no press coverage, no third-party reviews, no funding — all consistent with a very small team (likely <20 people) operating the 'boutique' model they describe
contradictions_with_recording.implication: The 'dedicated team per client' model is only scalable up to a small number of clients. Prospects should evaluate whether the level of service claimed can be maintained as the company grows or as their own needs increase.

contradictions_with_recording.claimed_in_recording: Presenter mentioned clients need to provide 3 custom domains (network login domain, auto-login domain, API domain)
contradictions_with_recording.public_data_shows: Public pricing page confirms: Starter Lead Distribution plan includes '1 tracking domain + 1 SSL + 1 API Domain'; Professional includes '5 tracking domains + 5 SSL + 1 API Domain'. The 3-domain requirement in the demo aligns with the Lead Distribution plans, not the simpler Affiliate Tracking plans.
contradictions_with_recording.implication: The domain structure confirms the prospect is being pitched the Lead Distribution System (higher-tier product), not the basic Affiliate Tracking Platform. This contextualizes the pricing as likely $1,000–$2,000/m, not $250–$750/m.

web_enrichment.analyst_summary: Getlinked (getlinked.io) is a bootstrapped, Israel/Bulgaria-based boutique SaaS company offering a white-label affiliate network and lead distribution platform primarily for Crypto/Forex and gambling performance marketers. Their most important competitive differentiator is not the software itself but the managed-service model: a dedicated technical team is bundled with the subscription at an accessible price point ($250–$2,000/m + $500 setup), letting solo operators and small teams run complex affiliate networks without hiring in-house developers. The key contradiction uncovered by this research is the pricing: the presenter withheld pricing during the demo as though it were custom/confidential, but a fully public pricing page exists with specific plan tiers — the prospect could have found this independently, and the demo pricing likely falls in the $1,000–$2,000/m range based on the Lead Distribution plan features demonstrated (3 custom domains, Finance Module, live support). The most significant stress signal is the complete absence of third-party reviews, press coverage, or any public social proof — in a market where Scaleo earns 4.8 on G2 and Everflow has strong brand recognition, Getlinked operates largely invisibly outside its referral network. A 13-minute dashboard loading failure during the sales demo is a material reliability concern that warrants technical due diligence. Overall, Getlinked appears to be a niche, well-featured product with a genuinely differentiated service model, but buyers should verify team capacity, contractual SLA commitments, and financial stability given the absence of any external validation of the company's claims.
